results.title: Viktor is rated "Excellent" with 4.5 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Is it perfect? No. There are growing pains. There are moments where things do not go as expected, and you have to work through them. But here is what has never wavered: the team behind this product.

The communication from the Viktor team has been exceptional, and I do not say that lightly. We live in a world where customer support means talking to an AI that misunderstands your question, sends you a link to an article you already read, and closes your ticket before your problem is solved. That is the new normal. Viktor is not that. Real people respond. They listen. They follow up. When I have had an issue, 

I have felt heard, not processed. Let me repeat. 

I HAVE FELT HEARD, NOT PROCESSED. [...] 1.   Software Company

### Written by the company

Viktor is the AI employee that lives inside Slack and Microsoft Teams. Instead of opening another tab, your team messages Viktor like a colleague and the work gets done across finance, operations, marketing, sales, support, and engineering. Viktor connects to the tools your business already runs on, including Salesforce, HubSpot, NetSuite, Shopify, Jira, and Notion. From solo founders to global teams, Viktor handles the work between the work, so people can focus on what matters most. Get started for free at viktor.com.See more

5-star

4-star [...] I HAVE FELT HEARD, NOT PROCESSED. 

The product itself is genuinely easy to use. I am not a developer. I am a teacher and a nonprofit founder who needed a technical co-founder but didn't have the budget for one. Viktor gave me that. I have built an entire platform ecosystem with it, and I did not need to know how to code to do it.

There are things I want to see improved. I have said so publicly and directly to the team. But that has not changed how I feel about where this is going. The bones are strong, the vision is real, and the people building it care.

I am staying with this product. That's why I leave a five-star review today.

results.title: Welcome, Viktor: The AI coworker for the modern workplace - Accel
results.content: Viktor is an AI employee who lives where the work is done - in your Slack or Microsoft Teams - and merges the worlds of collaboration and AI that have previously remained separate. Unlike most AI products used today, Viktor is intended to be used by teams rather than individuals. It works across the software teams already use and can perform a great and growing variety of tasks: analyze live business data, prepare a board-ready report, build an internal tool, launch a workflow, write code, call your customer, edit CRM data, plan an offsite, or coordinate work across systems. Anyone in a company can message Viktor like a colleague, add it to a team channel, and pick up a thread that a colleague started. Viktor also proactively notices tasks that need doing, offers help when needed, and

results.title: viktor.com: The AI employee that does the work, in Slack & Teams
results.content:
#### vs Alternatives

Before Viktor, we explored tools like Zapier, Make, Slack Workflow Builder, and custom scripts. Each worked for simple or structured tasks, but broke down when workflows became more dynamic or required real decision making. Traditional automation tools were too rigid, and standalone AI tools could not take real action within our systems. Viktor stood out because it combines reasoning with execution directly inside Slack, allowing us to handle complex workflows without needing to build and maintain our own infrastructure.

New User

#### What's great

It's all of the contextual awareness and action-taking capability you would want in an AI assistant with zero setup headache.

#### What needs improvement [...] PACT 
Zack Fediay

#### What's great

Blown away... first tool that did everything I asked it correctly the first time. Viktor diagnosed my page indexing issue, built a perfect dashboard in my Posthog account (also added new events I was missing), and proactively diagnosed an SSL issue I didn't know existed.

#### vs Alternatives

OpenClaw

Actual safety is accounted for, plus it lives in Slack. [...] #### What needs improvement

There are a few cases where it will respond to a message twice. I think there's a massive opportunity in having the tool ship with some preloaded frameworks around consolidating information around the rules you've given it to follow.

#### vs Alternatives

I've previously used another platform called Lindi, and while I loved that it worked in iMessage, its interface and overall workflow building experience were less than ideal. With Victor, setting up new flows and building new tools is ridiculously straightforward, and it works phenomenally with your team.

PACT 
Zack Fediay

results.title: Why is Microsoft teams so bad? : r/MicrosoftTeams
results.content:
Express-Gene1808

•3y ago• Edited 3y ago

I understand your frustrations with Microsoft Teams, and it's not uncommon to encounter some of the issues you've described. While the platform has a lot of potential for collaboration, it can have its quirks. To improve your experience, you might explore solutions like Twilio Microsoft Teams SMS, which can provide alternative communication methods. Additionally, make sure your Teams application is up to date, as Microsoft frequently releases updates to address performance issues. Clearing cache and ensuring your network connection is stable can also help. Sometimes, organization-specific settings or IT policies may contribute to the problems, so reaching out to your IT department for guidance is a good step.

 Reply  Share 

 more reply [...] - users trying to run teams from two devices and not understanding the difference when one is wired and the other is wireless

- MS not stating the obvious, for best performance 16GB RAM, SSD, i7 processor - preferably a dedicated video card.

- initially corporate internet pipe was maxed out during teams call (we monitored). went from 1GB to 10GB. Now no issues

- we use Verizon, which has a peering with MS - need to check your internet peering.

Teams app is inconsistent - with MS spending (wasting) time putting avatars in the app, I don't know who is setting priorities for what improvements need to occur.

Viktor is available in Slack and Microsoft Teams (soon). Not everyone can install a Slack bot. Not everyone uses Teams. Viktor ensures nobody on your team is locked out — regardless of their platform or admin permissions.

### No setup required

OpenClaw requires a developer to install, configure, and maintain. Viktor requires a Slack install, a Teams install, or a browser login. Anyone on your team — marketing, sales, support, leadership — can start using it in minutes with zero technical background.
